free_text: Full info. Compared other
Fission spectrum averaged cross sections of threshold
reactions (part I).
Moata reactor of UTR10 type with neutron
converter, see 'INC-SPEC'.
.Samples were irradiated at center of a neutron
converter consisting of an air-cooled hollow cylinder
of enriched U-Al alloy,2.5 cm dia.,10 cm long,contain-
ing 10 gram of U-235 (drawing and isotopic analysis in
JNE A/B 18,417(1964)), located in the internal graphite
reflector between the two core tanks of the reactor
Spectrum at the center of neutron converter conformed
closely to the U-235 fission spectrum. Fast flux was
4.14x10**10 n/cm2/sec. Daily variation was monitored by
the Al-27(n,alpha)Na-24 reaction. Fast flux from fuel
elements was 10**9 n/cm2/sec, less than 2.5% of fast
flux inside the converter. Measurements of fast flux by
5 monitor reactions agreed within cross section errors
and with calculated flux. Irradiation times were 3-6 hr
Spectrographically pure metals or oxides.
Activation
Data taken from J.Nucl.Energy,A/B,18(1964)417,
Table 3
KO.-Minor bib.Corrections.-
KO. -Added half-life in data of subent.004
HW. -New subentry 30143016 added (previous
subentry 70317006). Keyword monitor and monitor value
in common moved to subentries 002 - 015.-
HW.- 'SPA' replaced by 'FIS' in SF8 of
subents 02-15
BIB section updated
The absolute activities of samples were measured
with one or more of 4 Na-I(Tl) crystal spectrometers
by comparison with an absolutely calibrated standard of
the same isotope. Details and estimated accuracy in
J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964).
.Samples were placed directly on top of the Na-I
crystals during counting. Position was reproducible to
0.05 cm. Maximum variation in sample size was 0.10 cm.
HW.-Keywords monitor and monit-ref added
and monitor value added in common section.-
HW.- 'SPA' replaced by 'FIS' in SF8 of
reaction and monitor.-
The absolute activity of the sample was measured
with one or more of 4 Na-I(Tl) crystal spectrometers
whose efficiencies had been determined at 11 energies
using absolutely calibrated standards. Details and
estimated error in J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964).
.Samples were placed directly on top of the Na-I
crystals during counting, position was reproducible to
0.05 cm. Maximum variation in sample size was 0.10 cm.
Corrections were made for gamma sum-peaks and for
compton background when necessary.
Uncertainty in branching ratios has not
been included in error given. Assumed values of decay
parameters are given in J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964).

** Author questions isotopic purity because results
given here disagree with results obtained with sample
of natural composition using higher flux HIFAR
reactor.  See Exfor 30144.003. **

HW.- 'SPA' replaced by 'FIS' in SF8 of
reaction and monitor.-
Data given is total (n,p) cross
section for production of Co-58 because Co-58m decays
entirely to Co-58 ground state.
The absolute activities of samples were measured
with one or more of 4 Na-I(Tl) crystal spectrometers
by comparison with an absolutely calibrated standard of
the same isotope. Details and estimated accuracy in
J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964).
.Samples were placed directly on top the Na-I
crystals during counting. Position was reproducible to
0.05 cm. Maximum variation in sample size was 0.10 cm.
No corrections were necessary for thermal burn up of
either Co-58 or Co-58m because irradiation times were
short.
Uncertainty in branching ratios has not been
included in error given. Assumed values of decay
parameters are given in J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964).
HW. -Keywords monitor and monit-ref added
and monitor value added in common section.-
HW.- 'SPA' replaced by 'FIS' in SF8 of
reaction and monitor.-
The absolute activities of samples were measured
with one or more of 4 Na-I(Tl) crystal spectrometers
by comparison with an absolutely calibrated standard of
the same isotope. Details and estimated accuracy in
J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964).
.Samples were placed directly on top of the Na-I
crystals during counting. Position was reproducible to
0.05 cm. Maximum variation in sample size was 0.10 cm.
The partial cross section was determined from the
build up of Co-58 gamma activity in nickel samples.
No corrections were necessary for thermal burn up of
either Co-58 or Co-58m because irradiation times were
short.
Nothing said about errors
